text: perineal staple rectal resection for a full thickness, very large rectal prolapse in a frail elderly patient. Here we present a ninety year old female patient with a past medical history of atrial fibrillation, history of primary excision for squamous cell carcinoma from the lower leg in two thousand seventeen, local recurrence exercise in two thousand eighteen and further lymphadenectomy for recurrence in the left groin in two thousand eighteen. The patient presented with a full thickness rectal prolapse measuring ten centimeters which was having a significant negative impact on her quality of life. She was offered a perineal stapled prolapse resection, which is a relatively novel surgical procedure for treating external rectal prolapse with good clinical outcome. Frail patients who are unsuitable for prolonged abdominal procedures and can only endure a short procedure under regional anesthesia should be considered for perineal stapled rectal prolapse resection. PSPR is also beneficial for elderly patients with a large rectal prolapse, approximately eight to ten centimeters, and multiple comorbidities. In addition, PSPR has shown equally good clinical outcomes with young patients who want to avoid potential complication of nerve damage that is associated with the abdominal approach.

The patient is positioned in lithotomy with a slight Trendelenburg tilt. The surgeon reproduces the rectal prolapse by grasping its apex. Using a reloadable 100mm GIA linear stapler, the prolapse is resected longitudinally along the rectum at the three o'clock position. The surgeon waits at least one minute before firing the stapler due to the thickness and edema of the tissues.

The surgeon repeats the resection step at the nine o'clock position, creating anterior and posterior prolapsed rectal flaps. The anterior rectal flap is resected horizontally at the anorectal junction, parallel to but avoiding the dentate line.

The surgeon uses a reloadable endo-GIA linear stapling device to transect the posterior rectal flap. The flexible contour stapler allows for precise transection, and after the stapler is disengaged, the anal mucosa and neorectum spontaneously fall back into place following the complete resection.
